blog_title: Top 5 Religious Spots In Uttarakhand You Must Visit This Year With Your Spouse
seo_title: Top 5 Religious Spots In Uttarakhand You Must Visit This Year With Your Spouse
description: Discover the ultimate travel packing list for every adventure. Pack smart, light, and stress-free with essential tips .
body_content:
Published by travelxploria on Wed Jun 18 2025

Uttarakhand, known as ‘Dev Bhumi’ (Land of the Gods), is one of India’s most beautiful states and is renowned for its stunning landscapes and rich religious heritage.

Explore Uttarakhand’s Top 5 Religious Spots

Here are the top 5 religious spots in Uttarakhand that you must visit with your spouse this year, opting for one of the best Uttarakhand travel packages for couples curated by the best travel agency.

Badrinath Temple:

Located in the town of Badrinath in the Chamoli district, Badrinath Temple is one of the holiest shrines in India and is dedicated to Lord Vishnu. It is also one of the Char Dhams, the four pilgrimage sites that are considered the spiritual heart of the country.

The temple is nestled between the Nar and Narayan mountains and is flanked by the Alakananda River, offering breathtaking views of the Himalayas. The temple is known for its intricate carvings and vibrant decorations, and the aarti ceremony held every evening is a must-see.


Kedarnath Temple:

Another one of the Char Dhams, Kedarnath Temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is located in the Rudraprayag district. The temple is situated at an altitude of 3,583 meters above sea level, making it one of the highest Shiva temples in the world.

It is surrounded by the majestic Kedarnath Range and is only accessible by foot, making it a challenging yet rewarding pilgrimage. The temple is believed to be more than a thousand years old and is famous for its stunning architecture and the breathtaking views of the surrounding landscape, making it a part of even Uttarakhand honeymoon trip for newlyweds.

Haridwar:

Haridwar is one of the seven holiest places in Hinduism and is located on the banks of the Ganges River. It is famous for its ghats, where devotees come to take a dip in the holy river and cleanse themselves of their sins.

The Ganga Aarti held every evening at Har Ki Pauri is a mesmerizing sight, with thousands of lamps being floated on the river while devotees sing and dance in devotion. Haridwar is also home to several temples, ashrams, and markets selling religious paraphernalia.

Rishikesh:

Just a short drive from Haridwar, Rishikesh is known as the ‘Yoga Capital of the World’ and is a popular destination for spiritual seekers and adventure enthusiasts alike. The town is dotted with ashrams, temples, and yoga centers, making it a great place to unwind and connect with your inner self.
The Lakshman Jhula, a suspension bridge that spans the Ganges River, is a must-visit spot, and the evening Ganga Aarti at Triveni Ghat is a serene experience.

Gangotri Temple:

Gangotri Temple, dedicated to Goddess Ganga, is another one of the Char Dhams and is located in the Uttarkashi district. The temple is open for six months each year, from April to November, and is closed for the rest of the year due to heavy snowfall.

Uttarakhand offers a diverse range of religious sites that are sure to leave you and your spouse feeling spiritually rejuvenated and closer to each other. Whether you are looking for ancient temples, serene ghats, or tranquil ashrams, these five spots are a must-visit for anyone seeking a divine getaway.
